message:
feat(http): add graceful shutdown controller and coordinated server shutdown

Implements a comprehensive graceful shutdown system for TcpServer, enabling
coordinated shutdown from signal handlers, health endpoints, or external code.

## ShutdownController Integration

New TcpServer fields:
- shutdown_controller: Arc<ShutdownController> - coordinates shutdown signals

Public API additions:
- shutdown_controller() -> &Arc<ShutdownController>: access the controller
- subscribe_shutdown() -> ShutdownReceiver: get notified of shutdown
- shutdown(): initiate server shutdown (sets draining + notifies receivers)
- is_shutting_down(): check if shutdown has been initiated

## Graceful Shutdown Workflow

When shutdown() is called:
1. Sets draining flag to stop accepting new connections
2. Notifies all shutdown receivers via the controller
3. Accept loop exits and begins draining existing connections
4. Connections complete or timeout based on drain_timeout

## Integration Points

The shutdown controller enables:
- Signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGINT) to trigger clean shutdown
- Health check endpoints to return 503 during drain
- Background tasks to be notified and clean up
- Multiple receivers to coordinate shutdown activities

Method is idempotent - multiple calls are safe.

## Test Infrastructure

Added crates/fastapi-output/tests/integration_app_lifecycle.rs for
testing full application lifecycle including graceful shutdown scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
